{"id":"rawket-aeh","title":"Network Simulation: realistic link model for system tests","description":"Evolve the system test wire from instant/shared-clock to a realistic link model
with per-direction latency/bandwidth and independent clocks per side.

Design doc: designs/system-test-plan/network-simulation-design.md

## Problem
- Shared Clock::default() + clone means both Network stacks see identical time
- VirtualLink delivers frames instantly (zero latency, infinite bandwidth)
- Tests use magic `advance(5)` constants with no physical meaning
- BBR can't estimate bandwidth or detect congestion with zero RTT
- Pacing is untestable — frames arrive before pacing timer fires

## Solution
- LinkProfile with per-direction latency, bandwidth, jitter, loss
- Independent clocks per side (call Clock::default() twice)
- Delay queues in WireHarness gating frame delivery on sender clock
- Link-aware Pair helpers: advance_a_to_b(), advance_rtt(), drain_flight()
- 3-phase migration preserving all 148 existing tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] LinkProfile + DirectionProfile structs with preset profiles
- [ ] Independent clocks in Pair (clock_a, clock_b)
- [ ] Delay queues in WireHarness per direction
- [ ] Frame delivery gated on sender clock
- [ ] Pair advancement helpers replace magic constants
- [ ] All existing tests pass with LinkProfile::instant()
- [ ] At least one test uses realistic link profile (e.g. broadband)
- [ ] inter_packet_gap_matches_rate rewritten with link model","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"pop_shove_overflow@proton.me","created_at":"2026-03-05T06:30:45Z","created_by":"pop_shove_overflow","updated_at":"2026-03-05T06:30:45Z"}

{"id":"rawket-7am","title":"DelayQueue + frame delivery gating","description":"Add to wire.rs:
- DelayedFrame { data: Vec<u8>, delivery_time: u64 }
- DelayQueue wrapping BinaryHeap<Reverse<DelayedFrame>>, one per direction
- Add delay_a_to_b / delay_b_to_a + link + clock_a/clock_b to WireHarnessInner
- deliver_ready() method: moves frames from delay queue to rx_queue when delivery_time <= sender clock
- Pipe signaling only on actual delivery

Depends on: LinkProfile structs (for computing delivery_time)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"pop_shove_overflow@proton.me","created_at":"2026-03-05T06:34:04Z","created_by":"pop_shove_overflow","updated_at":"2026-03-05T06:34:04Z"}

{"id":"rawket-gp5","title":"Wire virtual_tx to use delay queues","description":"Modify virtual_tx() in wire.rs:
- After impairment processing (drop/corrupt), compute delivery_time from DirectionProfile
- Push to DelayQueue instead of directly to peer rx_queue
- Apply loss_rate check before enqueue
- Record send_time + delivery_time in CaptureBuffer metadata
- deliver_ready() called from rx_recv() (lazy) and from Pair helpers (eager)

With LinkProfile::instant(), behavior is identical to current (delivery_time = now).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"pop_shove_overflow@proton.me","created_at":"2026-03-05T06:34:08Z","created_by":"pop_shove_overflow","updated_at":"2026-03-05T06:34:08Z"}

- Add clock_a, clock_b fields to Pair (keep deprecated clock alias = clock_a.clone())
- Add link: LinkProfile field
- Add setup_pair_with_link(LinkProfile) constructor
- setup_pair() delegates to setup_pair_with_link(LinkProfile::instant())
- Pass clock_a to WireHarness, clock_b to WireHarness (for delay queue timing)
- establish_pair() and establish_pair_with_link() similarly

All existing tests use setup_pair() → instant profile → no behavior change.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"pop_shove_overflow@proton.me","created_at":"2026-03-05T06:34:12Z","created_by":"pop_shove_overflow","updated_at":"2026-03-05T06:34:12Z"}

{"id":"rawket-88l","title":"Pair advancement helpers","description":"Add methods to Pair in harness.rs:
- advance_a_to_b() — advance clock_b by A→B one-way delay (MSS-sized frame)
- advance_b_to_a() — advance clock_a by B→A one-way delay (ACK-sized frame)
- advance_rtt() — advance both clocks by full RTT
- drain_flight() — advance clocks enough to deliver all in-flight frames
- advance_both(ms) — advance both clocks by same amount (legacy compat)

Each helper also calls deliver_ready() to flush deliverable frames.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"pop_shove_overflow@proton.me","created_at":"2026-03-05T06:34:15Z","created_by":"pop_shove_overflow","updated_at":"2026-03-05T06:34:15Z"}

{"id":"rawket-aeh","title":"Network Simulation: realistic link model for system tests","description":"Evolve the system test wire from instant/shared-clock to a realistic link model
with per-direction latency/bandwidth and independent clocks per side.

Design doc: designs/system-test-plan/network-simulation-design.md

## Problem
- Shared Clock::default() + clone means both Network stacks see identical time
- VirtualLink delivers frames instantly (zero latency, infinite bandwidth)
- Tests use magic `advance(5)` constants with no physical meaning
- BBR can't estimate bandwidth or detect congestion with zero RTT
- Pacing is untestable — frames arrive before pacing timer fires

## Solution
- LinkProfile with per-direction latency, bandwidth, jitter, loss
- Independent clocks per side (call Clock::default() twice)
- Delay queues in WireHarness gating frame delivery on sender clock
- Link-aware Pair helpers: advance_a_to_b(), advance_rtt(), drain_flight()
- 3-phase migration preserving all 148 existing tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] LinkProfile + DirectionProfile structs with preset profiles
- [ ] Independent clocks in Pair (clock_a, clock_b)
- [ ] Delay queues in WireHarness per direction
- [ ] Frame delivery gated on sender clock
- [ ] Pair advancement helpers replace magic constants
- [ ] All existing tests pass with LinkProfile::instant()
- [ ] At least one test uses realistic link profile (e.g. broadband)
- [ ] inter_packet_gap_matches_rate rewritten with link model","status":"deferred","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"pop_shove_overflow@proton.me","created_at":"2026-03-05T06:30:45Z","created_by":"pop_shove_overflow","updated_at":"2026-03-05T06:38:35Z"}

new_value: Added DirectionProfile and LinkProfile structs with helper methods (serialization_delay_ms, one_way_delay_ms, rtt_ms) and preset constructors (instant, lan, broadband, lossy_wan) to wire.rs

Added DelayedFrame, DelayQueue (BinaryHeap min-heap), deliver_ready() method, and delay_a_to_b/delay_b_to_a/link/clock_a/clock_b fields to WireHarnessInner. WireHarness::new() defaults to LinkProfile::instant() with shared clock.

Added clock_a/clock_b/link fields to Pair (clock deprecated alias kept). setup_pair() uses shared clock for backward compat. setup_pair_with_link() and establish_pair_with_link() use independent clocks. WireHarness::with_link() constructor added. 148/148 tests pass.

virtual_tx now pushes frames to DelayQueue with delivery_time computed from DirectionProfile, then eagerly delivers ready frames. rx_recv does lazy delivery before reading. Corrupt impairment integrated into same flow. self_pipe_w added to VirtualLink. 148/148 tests pass.

Added Pair helpers: advance_a_to_b(), advance_b_to_a(), advance_rtt(), advance_both(ms), drain_flight(). WireHarness gained deliver_ready() and drain_flight(). DelayQueue gained max_delivery_time(). 148/148 tests pass.

Phase 1 complete. All 5 child tasks done: LinkProfile/DirectionProfile structs, DelayQueue, Pair with independent clocks + setup_pair_with_link, virtual_tx wired to delay queues, advancement helpers. 148/148 tests pass with no behavior changes.

Added pause_both() and resume_both() to Pair. 148/148 pass.
Added advance_a_to_b_for(frame_len) and advance_b_to_a_for(frame_len). Original advance_a_to_b/advance_b_to_a delegate to them with MSS/ACK defaults. 148/148 pass.

Switched delivery model from sender clock to receiver clock. drain_flight now advances clock_b for A→B and clock_a for B→A (receiver side). deliver_ready and rx_recv lazy delivery also use receiver clocks. Sender clocks are never modified by frame delivery. 148/148 pass.

virtual_tx now checks loss_rate after delay computation. Uses xorshift64 PRNG seeded from clock. Dropped frames are marked in CaptureBuffer (last_mut added). All presets except lossy_wan have loss_rate=0 so no behavior change. 148/148 pass.
virtual_tx now applies uniform [0, jitter_ms] random jitter to delivery_time. All presets have jitter_ms=0 so no behavior change. 148/148 pass.

{"id":"rawket-cd1y","title":"Strengthen system test assertions","description":"- type: epic
- priority: 2
- description: |
    Audit of all 148 system tests found 82 SOLID, 56 WEAK, and 10 INVALID tests.
    INVALID tests don't validate the requirement their name claims. WEAK tests have
    loose bounds, vacuous guards, or can't distinguish the mechanism under test from
    fallback behavior (e.g., RACK vs RTO).
    Strongest modules: keepalive (8/8), persist (7/8), mss (10/11), teardown (8/11).
    Weakest modules: bbr (2/15 solid), pacing (0/6 solid), rack_tlp (4/10 solid).
    This epic tracks fixing the INVALID tests first, then tightening WEAK assertions
    by cluster. Some fixes depend on the network simulation epic (rawket-aeh) for
    realistic timing measurement.
---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z"}

{"id":"rawket-e7dd","title":"Fix INVALID: state_machine::bad_checksum_dropped","description":"- type: bug
- priority: 1
- description: |
    Test name claims to validate bad checksum handling but rawket doesn't validate
    checksums. The test actually validates out-of-window segment dropping. Either:
    (a) rename to `oow_segment_dropped` and move to the correct conceptual group, or
    (b) implement checksum validation in the stack and write a real test.
---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z"}

{"id":"rawket-4hwm","title":"Fix INVALID: rto_rtt::karn_algorithm","description":"- type: bug
- priority: 1
- description: |
    Test advances clock past `rto_max_ms` before measuring the retransmit gap,
    making the gap assertion meaningless -- can't distinguish recovered RTO from
    inflated RTO. The second-phase clock advance should be just past `rto_min_ms`
    (not `rto_max_ms`) to verify the RTO recovered via timestamps.
---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z"}

{"id":"rawket-h5du4","title":"Fix INVALID: rto_rtt::rto_max_ceiling","description":"- type: bug
- priority: 1
- description: |
    With `max_retransmits=4` and 10 iterations each advancing by `rto_max_ms`,
    the socket is Closed before the assertion runs. The `if state != Closed` guard
    silently skips the check. Fix: use a higher `max_retransmits` or fewer iterations,
    and assert the check actually ran.
---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z"}

{"id":"rawket-0dk74","title":"Fix INVALID: fast_retransmit::window_update_not_dupack","description":"- type: bug
- priority: 1
- description: |
    Assertion allows both pass and fail (`retx_count <= 2`), documenting a known
    conformance gap rather than validating RFC 5681. Either:
    (a) fix the stack to not count window updates as dupacks, then tighten the assertion, or
    (b) rename the test to `window_update_counted_as_dupack` and mark it as a known deviation.
---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z"}

{"id":"rawket-o8g7t","title":"Fix INVALID: ooo::fin_in_ooo","description":"- type: bug
- priority: 1
- description: |
    Assertion accepts `State::Established` as valid (`b_state == CloseWait || Established`),
    making the FIN-processing check vacuous. After OOO drain with the FIN, B must be in
    CloseWait. Remove the `Established` alternative or add sufficient poll rounds to ensure
    the FIN is processed before asserting.
---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z"}

{"id":"rawket-m7heh","title":"Fix INVALID: rack_tlp::rack_reorder_window_adapts","description":"- type: bug
- priority: 1
- description: |
    Only checks liveness (Established state), no reorder window assertion. Should read
    `rack_reo_wnd_ms()` before and after reordering events and assert it increased.
    May need to expose `rack_reo_wnd_ms()` via test-internals if not already available.
---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z"}

{"id":"rawket-v2tkh","title":"Fix INVALID: rack_tlp::tlp_not_if_rto_sooner","description":"- type: bug
- priority: 1
- description: |
    Test claims to verify RFC 8985 section 7.3 (TLP suppressed when RTO fires sooner)
    but only checks liveness. Need to configure timers so RTO < TLP (e.g., rto_min=5ms
    with higher SRTT making TLP ~20ms), then verify the retransmit was RTO-driven
    (check timing matches RTO, not TLP interval) or that no TLP probe was scheduled.
---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z"}

{"id":"rawket-1vufs","title":"Fix INVALID: bbr::ack_splitting_no_cwnd_inflation","description":"- type: bug
- priority: 1
- description: |
    Never generates split ACKs. Sends 5KB through normal connection and checks cwnd is
    bounded. To properly test: inject multiple small ACKs for a single MSS-sized segment,
    then verify cwnd didn't grow more than it should for a single MSS of data ACKed.
---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z"}

{"id":"rawket-xppe7","title":"Fix INVALID: pacing tests (3 tests)","description":"- type: bug
- priority: 2
- description: |
    Three pacing tests are INVALID:
    - `inter_packet_gap_matches_rate`: never measures inter-packet timing
    - `probe_bw_up_increases_rate`: no rate measurement or phase check
    - `probe_bw_down_decreases_rate`: no rate measurement or phase check
    These require the network simulation epic (rawket-aeh) for realistic link timing.
    Until then, rewrite to at least check BBR phase transitions and pacing_rate values
    via test-internals accessors. Mark timing-dependent assertions as deferred.
- notes: |
    Depends on rawket-aeh for full fix. Partial fix possible now using phase/rate accessors.
---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z"}

{"id":"rawket-cmtf8","title":"Strengthen WEAK: BBR phase and internal state assertions","description":"- type: task
- priority: 2
- description: |
    11 of 15 BBR tests are weak smoke tests that never inspect `bbr_phase()`, `bw_lo`,
    `inflight_lo`, or actual cwnd growth patterns. Specific fixes:
    - `startup_phase_grows_cwnd`: sample cwnd at multiple points, assert monotonic increase
    - `startup_to_drain_transition`: assert `bbr_phase()` passes through Startup -> Drain -> ProbeBw
    - `probe_bw_up_down_cycle`: check phase is ProbeBw, inspect pacing gain changes
    - `probe_rtt_entry`: assert `bbr_phase() == ProbeRtt` after 10s advance
    - `loss_triggers_bw_lo_reduction`: read `bw_lo` before/after, assert decrease
    - `probe_rtt_cwnd_reduced`: assert `cwnd <= 4*MSS` while in ProbeRtt (check phase first)
    - `loss_halves_inflight_lo`: don't accept sentinel; assert actual halving
    - `bw_sample_window`: use a meaningful threshold, not `>= 1000`
    - `cwnd_target_includes_bdp`: compute expected BDP, compare against cwnd
    - `min_rtt_tracking`: assert value correlates with simulated RTT
    May need additional `test-internals` accessors for `bw_lo`, `inflight_lo`, `pacing_gain`.
---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z"}

{"id":"rawket-ccfp2","title":"Strengthen WEAK: RACK-TLP mechanism discrimination","description":"- type: task
- priority: 2
- description: |
    Several rack_tlp tests can't distinguish RACK-driven retransmits from RTO-driven ones:
    - `multiple_losses_detected`: use paused clock + precise advances to ensure retransmits
      happen before RTO, or check `rack_end_seq` advanced
    - `rack_updates_from_sack`: inspect `rack_end_seq` / `rack_xmit_ms` directly
    - `rack_reo_wnd_decay`: assert strict `<` (not `<=`), assert `reo_wnd_after_loss > 0`
    - `rack_loss_triggers_bbr_on_loss`: compare cwnd before/after, not just `<= 20*MSS`
---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z"}

{"id":"rawket-lgr1n","title":"Strengthen WEAK: loose bounds and vacuous guards","description":"- type: task
- priority: 3
- description: |
    Cross-cutting pattern of assertions that are technically correct but too loose
    to catch regressions:
    State machine:
    - `rst_with_invalid_seq`: also assert no error was set
    - `syn_retransmit`: count retransmitted SYNs, not just final state
    - `rst_in_window_challenge_ack`: verify challenge ACK seq/ack values, not just `any(ACK)`
    - `challenge_ack_rate_limit`: add lower bound `ack_count > 0`
    - `send_in_non_established`: require error return, don't accept success
    ECN:
    - `ce_marking_ece_cwr`: also check CWR on next data segment from sender
    - `ece_triggers_cwnd_reduction`: assert `cwnd_after <= cwnd_before` (not `*2`)
    Fast retransmit:
    - `partial_ack`: verify snd_una advanced
    - `triggers_bbr_loss`: compare cwnd before/after loss
    RTO/RTT:
    - `timestamps_override_karn`: tighter bounds on srtt/rto in zero-latency env
    - `rto_update_convergence`: handle min_srtt==0 case; use meaningful convergence check
---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z"}

{"id":"rawket-itqzl","title":"Strengthen WEAK: name vs. reality mismatches","description":"- type: task
- priority: 3
- description: |
    Tests where the name/comments claim something the assertions don't check:
    - `sack::most_recent_block_first`: actually check block ordering per RFC 2018 s4
    - `sack::ooo_buffer_limit`: check final ACK's SACK blocks, not union across all ACKs
    - `ooo::ooo_duplicate_seq`: verify D-SACK block, not just delivery
    - `ooo::ooo_segment_overlaps_rcv_nxt`: test partial overlap, not fully-old segment
    - `teardown::fin_piggybacked_on_data`: assert FIN+data in same segment
    - `teardown::half_close_data_delivery`: verify A's ACK advances past B's data
    - `teardown::syn_during_time_wait`: don't accept Closed as valid
    - `send_buf::flow_control_window_grows`: assert windows actually increase over time
    - `timestamps_paws::paws_drop_stale`: use in-window seq with stale TSval
    - `timestamps_paws::syn_bypasses_paws`: need prior ts_recent state
    - `isn::syn_collision_ack_numbers`: unwrap instead of `if let`
    - `isn::isn_epoch_collision`: reuse same Pair to test epoch-based ISN
    - `persist::persist_exponential_backoff`: verify inter-probe gap doubling","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T10:38:44Z"}
